recipes.title: Bamboo Shoot Coconut Curry
recipes.description: A cozy bamboo shoot curry with coconut milk, onion, garlic, curry spices, greens, and optional chickpeas or egg for protein.

photos.alt: Fresh peeled bamboo shoots ready to slice and cook
photos.caption: Fresh bamboo shoots: boil or blanch thoroughly first, then discard the water before cooking.

photos.alt: Bamboo shoot coconut curry prep with sliced shoots, coconut milk, onion, garlic, curry powder, greens, lime, and chickpeas
photos.caption: Prep checkpoint: sliced bamboo shoots with curry aromatics, coconut milk, greens, and protein booster ready.

photos.alt: Bamboo shoots simmering in coconut curry sauce with greens
photos.caption: Mid-cook checkpoint: curry base is fragrant, coconut milk is lightly simmering, and greens are just wilting.

photos.alt: Finished bamboo shoot coconut curry served with rice and lime
photos.caption: Finished dish: bamboo slices coated in a light coconut curry sauce, served warm with rice and lime.

ingredients.name: Chickpeas or boiled egg
ingredients.quantity: 150 g chickpeas or 2 eggs, optional protein booster

steps.title: Blanch the bamboo safely
steps.description: Slice bamboo shoots, boil 15–20 minutes, then drain and discard the water before using.
steps.visual: Slices should look pale, tender, and clean; water may look slightly yellow or bitter.

steps.title: Build the curry base
steps.description: Sauté onion and garlic in a little oil for 3–4 minutes, then stir in curry powder until fragrant.
steps.visual: Onion should be glossy and golden; spices should smell warm, not burnt.

steps.title: Simmer bamboo and coconut
steps.description: Add coconut milk, blanched bamboo shoots, and chickpeas or egg if using. Simmer 6–8 minutes.
steps.visual: Sauce should bubble gently and lightly coat the bamboo slices.

steps.title: Finish with greens and lime
steps.description: Add greens until just wilted, then finish with lime juice and seasoning.
steps.visual: Greens should be bright and soft; curry should look creamy but not heavy.

nutrition.note: Estimate assumes chickpeas as the optional protein booster; sodium depends on added salt. Bamboo shoots must be cooked thoroughly.

metadata.healthy_balance: Bamboo shoot fiber plus coconut fat, greens, and optional chickpeas or egg protein; serve with rice if you want a fuller meal.

visual_guide.prep_shot: Blanched bamboo shoot slices with onion, garlic, curry powder, coconut milk, greens, lime, and chickpeas ready.
visual_guide.mid_cook: Golden onion-garlic curry base with coconut milk gently simmering around bamboo slices.
visual_guide.finished: Creamy bamboo shoot curry with wilted greens, lime, and a small serving of rice.

metadata.nutrition_source: quick-healthy-recipes v1.3.0 estimate from input photo + recipe quantities
metadata.safety_note: Fresh bamboo shoots must be boiled or blanched thoroughly and the water discarded before eating.

recipes.title: Cassava Coconut Curry
recipes.description: A cozy, healthy cassava curry with coconut milk, onion, garlic, curry spices, and optional greens or chickpeas.

photos.alt: Fresh cassava roots on a kitchen surface before peeling and cooking
photos.caption: Fresh cassava: peel fully, cut into chunks, remove the woody center, and cook thoroughly.

photos.alt: Cassava ready to peel, cut, and boil for curry
photos.caption: Prep checkpoint: clean white cassava chunks with the tough center removed.

ingredients.name: Cassava / yuca
ingredients.quantity: 700 g, peeled, woody center removed, cut into chunks

ingredients.name: Chickpeas, egg, chicken, or fish
ingredients.quantity: 150 g cooked chickpeas or another protein, optional

steps.title: Prep cassava safely
steps.description: Peel the cassava completely, cut into chunks, and remove the tough woody center from each piece.
steps.visual: Chunks should look clean white with no brown skin or fibrous core.

steps.title: Boil until tender
steps.description: Boil cassava in salted water for 20–25 minutes, then drain.
steps.visual: A fork should slide in easily and edges should look slightly fluffy.

steps.title: Build the curry base
steps.description: Sauté onion and garlic in a little oil for 3–4 minutes. Add curry powder and stir until fragrant.
steps.visual: Onion should be glossy and golden; spices should smell warm, not burnt.

steps.title: Simmer and finish
steps.description: Add coconut milk, cassava, and greens or chickpeas if using. Simmer 5–8 minutes, then finish with lime.
steps.visual: Sauce should lightly coat the cassava chunks and greens should be just wilted.

visual_guide.prep_shot: Peeled cassava chunks with woody centers removed, next to onion, garlic, curry powder, coconut milk, and lime.
visual_guide.mid_cook: Cassava fork-tender and a golden onion-garlic curry base starting to simmer with coconut milk.
visual_guide.finished: Cassava chunks coated in a light coconut curry sauce with wilted greens and a squeeze of lime.

metadata.safety_note: Cassava must be peeled and cooked thoroughly; do not eat raw.
metadata.nutrition_source: quick-healthy-recipes v1.3.0 estimate from catalog photo + recipe quantities
metadata.healthy_balance: Carb-rich cassava with coconut fat, greens/fiber, and optional chickpeas or another protein to make it a complete dinner.

nutrition.note: Estimate assumes greens plus the optional chickpeas/protein booster; sodium depends on added salt.

recipes.title: Sautéed Swiss Chard with Garlic and Lemon
recipes.description: A simple, fast, healthy side dish that highlights fresh chard with garlic, lemon, and olive oil.

steps.title: Prep and separate
steps.description: Separate chard leaves from stems. Chop stems into 1/2-inch pieces. Roughly chop leaves.
steps.visual: Stems on one pile, leaves in another

steps.title: Sauté stems
steps.description: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Add stems and cook 5-6 minutes until glossy and softened.
steps.visual: Stems should be glossy and slightly tender

steps.title: Add garlic
steps.description: Add sliced garlic and red pepper flakes. Cook 1 minute until fragrant.
steps.visual: Garlic should be lightly golden, not browned

steps.title: Wilt leaves
steps.description: Add chopped leaves in batches, stirring until wilted. Add 1/4 cup water, cover, cook 5-7 minutes.
steps.visual: Leaves should be bright green and tender

steps.title: Finish
steps.description: Stir in lemon juice and zest. Season with salt and pepper.
steps.visual: Glistening, vibrant green with a fresh aroma

visual_guide.prep_shot: Fresh chard on the counter, stems and leaves visible before chopping
visual_guide.mid_cook: White stems glossy in the pan before adding leaves
visual_guide.finished: Tender green chard finished with lemon zest and garlic

metadata.nutrition_source: quick-healthy-recipes v1.3.0 estimate from catalog photo + recipe quantities
metadata.healthy_balance: Very vegetable-forward and fiber-rich; best as a side unless paired with eggs, beans, tofu, fish, or chicken.

photos.alt: Fresh Swiss chard with white stalks on a kitchen countertop
photos.caption: Fresh chard before cooking: separate thick stems from tender leaves.

photos.alt: Bunch of fresh chard ready to wash, trim, and chop
photos.caption: Prep checkpoint: wash well, then cut stems and leaves separately.

nutrition.note: Estimate assumes moderate seasoning; add eggs, beans, tofu, fish, or chicken for a fuller protein meal.

recipes.title: Warm Cinnamon Apples with Yogurt and Walnuts
recipes.description: A quick, healthy apple dessert-or-breakfast bowl with tender sautéed apples, creamy yogurt, and crunchy walnuts.

photos.alt: Fresh red, yellow, and green apples in a clear plastic bag
photos.caption: Fresh mixed apples: slice thinly so they soften quickly in the pan.

photos.alt: Apples ready to wash, core, and slice for a quick skillet recipe
photos.caption: Prep checkpoint: wash, core, and cut into thin wedges; peeling is optional.

ingredients.name: Apples
ingredients.quantity: 3 medium / about 450 g, cored and sliced

steps.title: Slice the apples
steps.description: Wash and core the apples, then cut them into thin wedges so they cook fast.
steps.visual: Thin slices with red and green skins still visible; no large chunks.

steps.title: Warm and soften
steps.description: Heat oil or butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add apples, cinnamon, and lemon juice. Cook 8–10 minutes, stirring often.
steps.visual: Apples should look glossy, lightly browned at the edges, and bend easily.

steps.title: Build the bowl
steps.description: Spoon yogurt into two bowls, top with warm apples, nuts, and a little honey if wanted.
steps.visual: Cool white yogurt with warm cinnamon apples piled on top and crunchy nuts scattered over.

visual_guide.prep_shot: Washed apples cored and sliced into thin wedges, skins on
visual_guide.mid_cook: Apple slices glossy and softening in a skillet with cinnamon
visual_guide.finished: Warm cinnamon apples over yogurt with walnuts and a small honey drizzle

input_photo.caption: Original input photo: fresh mixed apples in a clear plastic bag.

final_output.caption: Final recipe page output: dedicated recipe page with photos, quantities, and visual cooking guidance.

metadata.nutrition_source: quick-healthy-recipes v1.3.0 estimate from catalog photo + recipe quantities
metadata.healthy_balance: Fruit fiber plus Greek-yogurt protein and nuts for healthy fats; better as breakfast/snack than a full dinner.

nutrition.note: Sugar is mostly from apples and yogurt; skip honey to lower it slightly.
